name: Tastes of Chinatown: Neighborhood History and Food Tour
description: Enjoy delicious cuisine while learning the compelling stories of Chinatown’s history on this sensory tour featuring four savory dishes and one dessert.

name: Distillery Tour
description: Explore the production process of NYC's oldest whiskey distillery, from milling and mashing to fermentation and distilling. The tour includes a visit to the barrel room for a guided tasting of core spirits.

name: NYCR Craft Night
description: Bring your current crafting project and work on it with other artists, crafters, and makers of all kinds. Projects range from costumes and woodworking to electronics and visual arts.

name: Whiskey Tasting Class
description: Learn how to nose, taste, and evaluate whiskey like a pro during this expert-led session covering production and barrel aging. The class includes a flight of five premium hand-crafted whiskeys.

name: A Christmas Carol, Oy! Hanukkah, Merry Kwanzaa, Happy Ramadan
description: This toy-puppet theater extravaganza is a new take on Charles Dickens' classic with Old World accents and New World inclusiveness.
short_name: A Christmas Carol, Oy! Hanukkah, Merry Kwanzaa, Happy Ramadan

name: Family Bowl
description: A family-friendly holiday bowling session open to all ages with no cover charge.

name: Play at BCM
description: Come explore some of BCM's most-loved exhibits, including toddler-favorite Totally Tots and the miniature shops of World Brooklyn.

name: Brooklyn Navy Yard: Past, Present & Future
description: This permanent exhibition explores the Brooklyn Navy Yard's history from its 1801 founding to its modern role as an innovation hub. Visitors can view artifacts and historical narratives spread across three floors of a restored 1858 residence.

name: Friday Night Cooking Party Evento x Bucketlisters
description: A collaborative hands-on cooking social hosted by Evento and Bucketlisters. Enjoy an evening of Italian culinary techniques, drinks, and party vibes to kick off the weekend.
short_name: Friday Night Cooking Party Evento x Bucketlisters

name: Pen Pals
description: This play tells the story of two women who have been best friends for fifty years through letters, featuring Melissa Gilbert and Veanne Cox. Five percent of ticket sales are donated to Susan G. Komen for breast cancer research.

name: Friday Nights
description: Experience one of NYC's premier rooftop nightlife events featuring three floors of music and dancing. Guests can enjoy French dining and a dynamic atmosphere with optional bottle service.

name: Reggaeton Fridays @ The DL
description: Dance to the best Reggaeton beats at this multi-level club event. The night features high-energy Latin music across three floors with table reservations available.

name: DIY Tapestry Weaving for Beginners
description: Learn how to dress a loom and create small tapestry-style weavings that can be used as coasters or wall hangings. Each student receives a simple homemade loom to keep.

name: Reggaeton Saturdays @ The DL
description: Keep the weekend energy high with a dedicated Reggaeton night in the heart of the city. Enjoy eclectic music and unforgettable vibes across multiple levels.

name: Rooftop Saturdays
description: Spend your Saturday night on a dynamic rooftop overlooking the city. This event promises a snapshot of the city's rhythm with premium bottle service and diverse music.

name: Bottomless Brunch
description: Enjoy bottomless mimosas and bellinis along with a $30 TOM card to spend at any of the market's food vendors. This weekend tradition offers the perfect way to sample editor-approved concessions.

name: Tarot 101: Learn How to Do Tarot Readings
description: Discover the meanings of all 78 cards in the deck and practice intuitive shuffling techniques to perform your own readings. This class includes a classic Rider-Waite Tarot deck and a bundle of sage.
short_name: Learn How to Do Tarot Readings

name: 2026 Vision Board Crafting & Personal Tarot Readings
description: Set your intentions for the New Year by crafting a vision board while receiving a private tarot reading to clarify your current energy. All basic crafting supplies are provided.
short_name: 2026 Vision Board Crafting & Personal Tarot Readings

name: Writers Group
description: A collaborative space for writers to share their current projects and receive constructive feedback from peers. This group supports various genres and levels of experience.

name: Issues and Action Discussion Circle
description: A weekly gathering to discuss social justice issues and current events from an ethical perspective. Participants explore ways to take meaningful action in their communities.

name: Watercolor Exploration
description: Explore watercolor's unique properties through demonstrations of wet-on-wet techniques, layering, and building from light to dark. Students will have time to experiment with landscapes and abstract works.

name: Morning Food Scrap Drop-off
description: Bring fruit, vegetable, and coffee ground scraps to be turned into nutrient-rich compost used throughout the park. Meat, fish, citrus, and compostable cutlery are not accepted.

name: Winter Workshops with A Rosie Day
description: Join A Rosie Day for cozy crafting, skating, and seasonal magic. This free workshop is open to all ages, even for those who do not wish to skate.

name: The Open Mic Downstairs @ Frigid New York
description: A supportive weekly open mic where performers get seven minutes to try anything from performance art and comedy to music and dance. The event focuses on building an attentive and welcoming community.

name: 222 Tuesdays
description: Visit the historical loft at 222 Bowery to browse artist-curated books, listen to records, and explore the famous Burroughs bedroom. This weekly open house is a space for music and conversation.

name: Tuesday Night Jazz with Danny Jonokuchi
description: Enjoy an intimate evening of jazz and wine with Danny Jonokuchi & The Revisionists every other Tuesday night. Hear award-winning swing tunes and dance the night away in the Wine Cave.

name: Domino Park Trivia
description: Join Other Half Brewing for a trivia night at their Domino Park location. Test your knowledge while enjoying local craft beer in a lively brewery setting.

name: Soul Records Laboratory
description: Join Soul in the Horn for "Soul Records Laboratory," a weekly music series featuring up-and-coming DJs. Dance to electrifying jams with views of the NYC skyline while enjoying cocktails and food.

name: Weekend Evenings this Holiday Season
description: Our festive pop-up bar will be open from 6pm-11pm Thursday through Sunday nights, serving over-the-top holiday cocktails and mocktails, twinkle lights galore, and zero laptops in sight. Expect _pawsitively_ joyful tunes for the season. We'll be serving a sparkly, silly holiday vibe fueled by peppermint, glitter, and good cheer.

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Brooklyn Bread - Carroll Gardens Location)
description: Help transport donated items from Brooklyn Bread's Carroll Gardens store to the CHiPS soup kitchen. Volunteers play a vital role in recovering surplus food for distribution to the local community.

name: Evening Food Scrap Drop-off
description: Bring fruit, vegetable, and coffee ground scraps to be turned into nutrient-rich compost used throughout the park. Meat, fish, citrus, and compostable cutlery are not accepted.

name: ZOO
description: A docufiction hybrid exploring the serious and surreal story of a Seattle-area zoophile community. This haunting film refuses to compromise style for headline urgency.

name: The Flying Luna Clipper
description: A curated screening at the collective-run microcinema Spectacle in Brooklyn, featuring rare and under-the-radar cult programming.

name: Salsa Thursdays
description: Get your salsa on for free every Thursday with a dance lesson from Marlene Veras followed by a full-on dance party. Music is provided by DJ Ray Suave and a live percussionist.

name: Game Center Playtest Thursdays
description: Bring your work-in-progress games, including digital projects, board games, or VR, to get feedback from fellow game makers. Open to all designers, players, and game enthusiasts.

name: New Sanctuary Organizing Meeting
description: Learn how to protect neighbors and develop ways to face threats to immigrant communities through court witnessing and accompaniment programs.

name: Kill the Moonlight
description: An underground cult classic screening at the Spectacle microcinema collective in Williamsburg.

name: Book a School Tour
description: Hands-on guided tours based on age-appropriate lesson plans for grades K-12, encouraging students to think critically and creatively about food. Offered Thursdays & Fridays.

name: Masks On Craft Night
description: All the fun of a regular craft night with the added safety of a masks-required environment. This session is specifically for those who prefer a more COVID-safe crafting space.

name: Weeknights
description: Part of the collective-run microcinema's usual array of unique programming, showcasing the best of underground cinema.

name: Friday Night Vibes at Time Out Market
description: Start your weekend off right at Time Out Market New York’s stunning rooftop. Friday Night Vibes gets the party going with tunes from DJ Stretch, specialty cocktails and bites from various kitchens.

name: Standing Slightly Outside: Franklin Furnace, 1976—2026
description: A gallery show marks the 50th anniversary of Franklin Furnace, a vital New York arts space and archive.

name: Friday Nights @ The Delancey Nightclub
description: Join the city’s lively night owls on the dance floor for an electrifying night of music and drinks. Experience one of the most dynamic nightclub atmospheres in the city.

name: Reggaeton Fridays @ The Delancey's Lower Level
description: Dive into a world of high-energy Latin vibes and pulsating beats in the venue's lower level. This event is a go-to for Reggaeton fans looking for an unforgettable night out.

name: RuPaul’s Drag Race Watch Party Hosted by Lailah Lancing
description: Join host Lailah Lancing for a weekly fierce watch party featuring big screens, themed cocktails, and tacos. Participate in an interactive prediction game for a chance to win free drinks and prizes.
short_name: RuPaul’s Drag Race Watch Party Hosted by Lailah Lancing

name: Food Rescue Pickup (Brooklyn Bread - Park Slope Location)
description: Assist with the collection of fresh bread donations from Brooklyn Bread's Park Slope location. This simple task ensures that quality food reaches individuals experiencing food insecurity through CHiPS' services.

name: Wake Up & Dance with Suzi Shelton
description: Award-winning children's musician Suzi Shelton leads a joyful concert series for young dancers and their families.

name: Cathedral of New Emotions
description: A unique cinematic experience at the Spectacle microcinema in Brooklyn, part of their curated film series.

name: The Best of Me
description: Part of the Best of Spectacle 2025 series, highlighting top underground and rare films screened at the microcinema.

name: Despiser
description: A late-night cult screening at Brooklyn's collective-run microcinema, exploring the fringes of independent cinema.

name: Spotlight Saturdays: Noise Complaint
description: DJ LSJ presents a late-night party featuring hip-hop, RnB, Afro beats, and Latin mixes. Enjoy drink specials from Cazadores Tequila and jello shots on the rooftop.

name: Spotlight Saturdays with DJ LSJ
description: Celebrate Saturday night with live DJ sets by DJ LSJ, featuring specialty drinks and great views of the NYC skyline.

name: Saturday Nights @ The Delancey Nightclub
description: Immerse yourself in the ultimate Saturday night experience with pulsating music and a lively crowd. It is the perfect destination for an epic evening of dancing and drinks.

name: Safaera Saturdays @ The Delancey's Lower Level
description: Experience a night of non-stop energy featuring the best in Latin and Reggaeton music. Get lost in the rhythm and enjoy the drinks in a vibrant underground setting.

name: Tom D's Big New York Show
description: Comedian and certified NYC tour guide Tom Delgado leads an entertaining tour through different aspects of New York history. The show features special guests, trivia with prizes, and storytelling.
short_name: Tom D's Big New York Show

name: Brooklyn Navy Yard: World War II Tour
description: Explore the history of the Navy Yard during its peak production years in World War II. This walking tour visits historical sites and shares stories of the sailors and workers who served there.
short_name: Brooklyn Navy Yard: World War II Tour

name: The Fat Cats Youth Orchestra Open Workshop and Rehearsals
description: Weekly open workshops and rehearsals for the Fat Cats Youth Orchestra, focusing on mentorship and musical excellence.
short_name: The Fat Cats Youth Orchestra Open Workshop and Rehearsals

name: Children of the Stones
description: A curated screening of the cult classic television series at Spectacle microcinema.

name: Free One Hour Outdoor Yoga in La Plaza
description: A welcoming multi-level yoga class open to everyone in an outdoor setting. This session focuses on mindfulness, body breath awareness, and stretching under the trees.
short_name: Free One Hour Outdoor Yoga in La Plaza

name: Short(er) Slavic Novels Book Club with Arya
description: This meeting focuses on Ivan Klima's "Love and Garbage," a novel set in Prague that explores love and conscience under totalitarianism. This book club focuses on Slavic literature under 300 pages.

name: First Mondays: Readings of New Works In Progress
description: Organized by Sarah Schulman, this monthly program features writers from various generations previewing new works-in-progress devoid of censorship.
short_name: Readings of New Works In Progress

name: Monsters
description: An anthology of televised spooks created by Richard P. Rubenstein, featuring goopy makeup effects and talent from post-no wave New York directors.

name: Frantic! Free Show
description: A high-energy free comedy showcase featuring Matthew Broussard, Carolina Montesquieu, and more.

name: Improv for Communication Skills
description: Use improvisational exercises to build professional communication skills like active listening, presenting, and thinking on your feet. No prior comedy or acting experience is required for this workshop.

name: Literary Black Book Club with Genay
description: The group will discuss "The Wilderness" by Angela Flournoy, which follows five Black women through twenty years of friendship. This club focuses on contemporary fiction primarily written by Black women.

name: Schmoedipus
description: A curated screening at Spectacle, showcasing under-the-radar and unique independent programming in Brooklyn.

name: Arctic Reverie
location: The Sheen Center for Thought & Culture
description: Artist Leslie Sobel presents paintings documenting the beauty and fragility of the High Arctic based on residencies in Svalbard. The works aim to capture the sacred nature of landscapes transforming due to climate change.

name: Taking Inspiration from The Best American Short Stories with Cora Lewis
description: Explore the craft of short fiction by analyzing selections from The Best American Short Stories to find creative inspiration.
short_name: Taking Inspiration from The Best American Short Stories

name: Close Encounters: Writing Intimacy and Connection with Danielle Lazarin
description: This workshop focuses on the art of writing authentic emotional and physical intimacy in fiction through craft exercises.

name: Trivia @ Parklife Hosted by Hope Morawa
description: Test your knowledge at this free weekly trivia night hosted by Hope Morawa. Compete for fun prizes including free drinks, tacos, and tickets to littlefield.

name: PACKRAT: The Quest por la Abundancia
description: A visually crafted puppet-forward play that explores themes of home, belonging, and responsibility during the climate crisis. The production is inspired by the Indigenous Comcáac people of northern Mexico.
short_name: PACKRAT: The Quest por la Abundancia

name: DJ Nights at Skate
description: Join DJ girlcrush and guest DJ RAISA for a night of high-energy music at the ice rink. These sessions feature a mix of house, techno, and hyperpop to keep you moving on and off the ice.

name: Silver Arrow Band
description: A high-energy live music showcase from party starters designed to help you plan your next event.

name: Winter Virtual Poetry Intensive: Amateur Poetics
location: The Poetry Society of New York
description: A six-week poetry workshop exploring the concept of 'amateur' poetics, focusing on writing for pleasure and interest rather than profit.

name: The Moth Storyslam
description: An open-mic storytelling competition where anyone can share a five-minute story.

name: Everyday Hauntings: Writing Contemporary Gothic Fiction with JD Scott
description: Examine elements of modern Gothic fiction and learn how to incorporate uncanny atmospheres into contemporary settings.

name: Writing Short Fiction: Where to Begin with Amy Silverberg
description: An introductory workshop designed to help writers find their voice and start crafting short stories covering character, plot, and prose.

name: Yoga & Wellness Session
description: Join a community wellness session where attendees are encouraged to bring their own mat. Walk-ins are welcome with credit card payment at the door.

name: Ches Smith Quartet
description: Percussionist Ches Smith leads a quartet with Mary Halvorson, Liberty Ellman, and Nick Dunston. The group performs different music every night during this residency.

name: Fantasy & Fables Book Club
description: A recurring monthly club focusing on the fantasy and romantasy genres, specifically highlighting debut novels. Attendees can enjoy a discount on drinks at the bar during the discussion.

name: Community Access Thursdays
description: Explore the Museum's most-loved exhibits during these community access hours held every Thursday afternoon.

name: New Book Book Club with Sarah
description: This month the club discusses David Szalay's 2025 Booker Prize-winning novel "Flesh." This event is currently listed as sold out.

name: No Sex Last Night (Double Blind)
description: A screening of Sophie Calle and Greg Shephard's experimental road movie at Brooklyn's Spectacle microcinema.

name: Yamavicascope: the Short Films of Isao Yamada
description: A retrospective of short films by Isao Yamada, showcasing the artist's unique visual style and poetic storytelling.
short_name: the Short Films of Isao Yamada

name: Shared Bill: Isa Spector: Derek Smith
description: A double feature presenting a domestic fantasy movement piece and a spectacular duet blending song and dance.
short_name: Shared Bill: Isa Spector: Derek Smith

name: Themed Thursdays hosted by Milk
description: Join drag performer and figure skater Milk for a themed night on the ice with curated weekly themes. The best on-theme costume wins a pair of SKATE admissions, and Milk performs at 7:30 pm.

name: Anatol’s Camera + Rare Photos
description: View rare photographs of Anatol Josepho, the inventor of the photobooth, and an original camera from 1925. This display provides an intimate look at the origin of analog photography technology.

name: Celebrity Prints
description: Discover one-of-a-kind celebrity photostrips featuring stars such as Michael Jackson and Serena Williams. These candid snapshots capture a unique, analog perspective on fame and identity.

name: The Photobooth Technicians Project
description: An exhibit featuring a unique accordion-fold book that explores photobooth history through interviews with 49 global technicians. The project highlights the manual labor and culture that keeps vintage booths running.

name: Stop Motion Film & Miniatures
description: View 'The Photobooth Technician', a stop-motion film by Rachel Rowe that celebrates the art of the photobooth. The exhibition also features intricate miniatures created by the puppetry artist.

name: The New York Neo-Futurists Present: Best of the Infinite Wrench
description: A fast-paced collection of short plays that attempt to perform 30 plays in 60 minutes, blending high-concept art with low-brow humor.

name: Prospect Park Board Gaming Club
description: An evening dedicated to tabletop gaming and social strategy. Players of all skill levels are welcome to join for a night of board games and community fun.

name: God Is in Your Typewriter
description: A meta-theatrical exploration of the life and psyche of poet Anne Sexton, interweaving historical fact with surrealist drama and music.

name: La MaMa Kids: Don Quixote Takes New York
description: An interactive puppetry experience for children that reimagines the adventures of Don Quixote in the setting of modern New York City.
short_name: La MaMa Kids: Don Quixote Takes New York

name: Seanchoíche New York: Childhood
description: An evening of storytelling themed around "Childhood," bringing the popular Dublin-founded event series back to Brooklyn.

name: The Passing
description: A midnight-adjacent screening of Bill Viola's experimental work exploring the boundaries between life and death.

name: Wto/99
description: An immersive archival documentary reanimating the street protests of the 1999 Seattle WTO summit. Includes a Q&A with Editor/Producer Alex Megaro.

name: Hello Dankness
description: A curated screening by the Spectacle microcinema collective in Brooklyn, showcasing unique and under-the-radar independent programming.

name: Ask A Fuckboy
description: Female comics present unhinged dating stories, and a panel of male comedians ("fuckboys") weigh in. It combines the irreverence of Call Her Daddy with indulgent storytelling.

name: 2024 Baby Meetup at Wild East
description: A casual weekly Friday meetup for parents, families, and babies at Wild East Brewing.

name: Old Yorkers
description: A sketch and improv show featuring performers bringing unique New York perspectives to the stage.

name: QUEER TIME: Marlon Riggs / Sedat Pakay / Tourmaline & Sasha Wortzel
description: A powerful program exploring the interplay of desire, race, and identity from a Black Queer perspective through testimonials and performance. It features Marlon Riggs's groundbreaking TONGUES UNTIED along with intimate portraits of James Baldwin and Marsha P. Johnson.
short_name: Marlon Riggs / Sedat Pakay / Tourmaline & Sasha Wortzel

name: Doclisboa: Želimir Žilnik's Eighty Plus
description: A jazz pianist returns to his home in Serbia after sixty years to inherit a neglected mansion in this humanistic and tender film. The screening explores post-socialist restitution and the unexpected chapters of life in old age.

name: All That Fall
description: Directed by Joanne Akalaitis, this experimental production of Samuel Beckett’s first play with a female protagonist creates a visual and sonic landscape of ordinary life saturated with morbid humor. The performance follows Maddie Rooney's journey to a rail station as she encounters various characters and reflects on decline and death.

name: Pianos: Free Entry before 11PM w/ RSVP
description: A late-night DJ and dance event at the Pianos Main Bar with free entry for guests who RSVP before 11 PM.

name: Happy Hour at Gowanus Gardens
description: Enjoy weekday happy hour specials at Gowanus Gardens, featuring craft cocktails and a colorful outdoor patio. The event runs every Monday through Friday from 3 PM to 7 PM.

name: Street Food City
description: This exhibition celebrates generations of immigrant street food entrepreneurs in New York City and the diverse dishes they introduced to the culinary landscape.

name: World Food Photography Awards
description: A powerful photography exhibition co-curated with the WFPA to capture vibrant street food culture and traditions from across the globe.

name: The Voice of Hind Rajab
description: This powerful documentary film tells the story of a Palestinian girl whose calls for help reached the world. It provides an intimate and moving portrait of survival and loss.

name: The Parent Trap (1998)
description: Identical twins meet for the first time at summer camp and hatch a plan to reunite their parents.

name: Yojimbo
description: Akira Kurosawa's masterpiece featuring a wandering samurai caught between two rival gangs.

name: The Met Live in HD: I Puritani
description: Experience a live high-definition broadcast of the Metropolitan Opera's production of Bellini’s masterpiece, I Puritani.
short_name: The Met Live in HD: I Puritani

name: The Art of Bonsai
description: Learn the ancient art form of growing miniature trees in containers from a bonsai professional. Students take home their own bonsai tree, container, and soil after learning maintenance and design techniques.

name: Wham! Bam! Thank You Ma’am! A Bowie Dance Party
description: A dance party celebration of David Bowie immediately following the live band karaoke event.
short_name: Wham! Bam! Thank You Ma’am! A Bowie Dance Party

name: Child of Peach
description: A wild fantasy screening at Spectacle, exploring the boundaries of cult world cinema.

name: The Animations of Le Dernier Cri
description: A program of wild, graphic animations from the Marseille-based art collective Le Dernier Cri.
short_name: The Animations of Le Dernier Cri

name: Live Music Performance
description: An evening of live music featuring talented artists at the Bowery Ballroom.

name: Monthly Meditation Retreat
description: Monthly Nyingma Buddhist meditation retreat hosted by monks from the Padmasambhava Buddhist Center.

name: The Mic at The Second City
description: An open mic event where new and established comedians test out their latest material.

name: EARTH TIME: Cauleen Smith / Otolith Group / Miatta Kawinzi
description: An audiovisual collection of films meditating on geological time, tectonic forces, and landscapes as sites of refuge and healing. These works examine the 'infrastructural unconscious' of Southern California alongside the historical links between Liberia and the United States.
short_name: Cauleen Smith / Otolith Group / Miatta Kawinzi

name: HAUNTED TIME: Witherspoon / Campbell / Owens / Julien
description: Short films that investigate memory, grief, and the erasure of cultural spaces through different lens. The program includes Keisha Rae Witherspoon's 'T' and Isaac Julien's lyrical exploration of Langston Hughes.

name: Open Level Flying Trapeze
description: Participants of all skill levels, ages 5 to adult, can experience the thrill of flight in this open-level trapeze class. Professional instructors guide students through foundational flying and acrobatic techniques in a supportive environment.

name: Mulchfest
description: Recycle your holiday tree and receive a discount for ice skating admission. Trees are chipped into mulch to nourish park greenery in collaboration with the NYC Department of Parks & Recreation.

name: Two Lesbians Walk Into A Bar...
description: A dark comedy play that follows the intense bond between a young emerging comic and her idol as they navigate romantic and professional lives. This show explores ambition, digital fame, and gender politics within the world of stand-up comedy.
short_name: Two Lesbians Walk Into A Bar...

name: Coffee with Comrades
description: Informal social meetups held across different NYC neighborhoods. Meet fellow socialists, discuss local issues, and build community in a relaxed setting.

name: St. John's Watch Party
description: Watch the St. John's Red Storm take on Providence, Creighton, and Villanova. Enjoy drink specials on Game Theory, the official pilsner of St. John's Athletics, while you cheer on the Johnnies.

name: Weekend Evenings Holiday Pop-Up Bar
description: A festive pop-up bar serving over-the-top holiday cocktails and mocktails. Expect joyful tunes and a sparkly holiday vibe for you and your furry friends.

name: Matinee Disco @ Joyface
description: An afternoon disco dance party at Joyface featuring retro beats and a nostalgic vibe.

name: Hamnet
description: Paul Mescal stars as William Shakespeare opposite Jessie Buckley in Chloé Zhao’s historical drama following the tragic early life of the playwright's son.

name: Accelerated TV Studio
description: Learn essential skills for television studio environments, including camera operation, lighting setup, and live broadcasting. This certification course is designed for individuals with some production background.

name: The Art of Video Editing
description: Learn artistic concepts in film and video editing such as Walter Murch’s Rule of Six, Montage Theory, and Continuity Editing. This class is suitable for beginning editors and filmmakers wanting to explore postproduction possibilities.

name: Austra
description: Electronic music project Austra performs a 21+ show in Brooklyn. Doors open at 6:30 PM.

name: Eric Reprid
description: Canadian artist Eric Reprid performs an 18+ late show at Baby's All Right.

name: The Lasting Legend of Luqman
description: An afternoon gathering and musical tribute dedicated to the enduring legacy and memory of Luqman. Join the community for a special performance and reflection.

name: Transit Tots
description: This early morning program for families with children ages 18 months to 3 years features story-time and crafts. It is designed to engage the youngest transportation fans through imaginative play.

name: Movers and Makers: Family Tour and Workshop
description: This monthly family workshop series includes a tour of select exhibitions followed by a hands-on art project. The program is designed for families with children ages 5 to 14.

name: Stereo Mandrax (FREE w/ RSVP): Danny Arakaki + Bad Trips + Ka Baird x Joanna Mattrey x Qasim Naqvi
description: A special free showcase featuring a collaborative and experimental lineup. Performers include Danny Arakaki, Bad Trips, and a unique trio consisting of Ka Baird, Joanna Mattrey, and Qasim Naqvi.
short_name: Danny Arakaki + Bad Trips + Ka Baird x Joanna Mattrey x Qasim Naqvi

name: Gotham Writers Workshop: Memoir Writing
description: Author Cullen Thomas walks participants through techniques for bringing personal life stories to the page with narrative finesse.

name: Suzuki Prospective Family Orientation – January 11
description: This required in-person orientation is the first step for families planning to enroll in the Brooklyn Conservatory of Music’s Suzuki Program. The session features a presentation by the program director followed by a Q&A session.

name: The Rock and Roll Playhouse plays Music of David Bowie for Kids
description: A special "Bowie Birthday Bash" concert for kids featuring the iconic hits of David Bowie.
short_name: The Rock and Roll Playhouse plays Music of David Bowie for Kids

name: Come Back to Earth: Mac Miller Tribute
description: A heartfelt live tribute performance celebrating the music and memory of Mac Miller.
short_name: Come Back to Earth: Mac Miller Tribute

name: From Brooklyn to Palestine: Militarism, Colonialism, and the Navy Yard
description: A presentation examining the historical and political connections between Brooklyn's Navy Yard and international issues of militarism. The event focuses on the intersection of local history and global human rights.
short_name: Militarism, Colonialism, and the Navy Yard

name: gobbinjr
description: An indie pop performance featuring the unique songwriting of gobbinjr.

name: Major Moment with Iara
description: Boston-based pop metal band Major Moment brings an intense and cinematic sound to the stage with support from IARA.

name: Junhee – Into the Ocean
description: Korean artist Junhee brings the "Into the Ocean" US Tour to Brooklyn for an all-ages performance. Expect an evening of soulful melodies and captivating stage presence.

name: The Closties Variety Hour
description: Kashia Kancey presents a variety hour performance as part of the Independent Works series.

name: Quakerism 101
description: A monthly discussion about different aspects of Quaker faith, practice, and history.

name: Faithfulness Groups Workshop
description: An introduction to the practice of faithfulness groups, which help participants discern divine guidance and provide mutual spiritual support.

name: Diva Diva: Physical Therapy, Shaun J. Wright, Charlene
description: A high-energy Sunday session focused on club sounds and vibrant dancefloor energy.
short_name: Physical Therapy, Shaun J. Wright, Charlene

name: LayeRhythm: Music and Dance
description: A collaborative showcase where freestyle dancers and a live band engage in improvisational performance.

name: The Big Niko & GiGi Puppet Singalong
description: Nikolai Moderbacher creates a family-friendly experience blending live music and interactive puppetry for young children.
short_name: The Big Niko & GiGi Puppet Singalong

name: Schneider Concerts Presents: Dana Kelley, January Preconcert Talk
description: Violist Dana Kelley explores works being performed during the Schneider Concerts 2025-2026 season. This pre-concert talk provides in-depth context for the afternoon performance.

name: Schneider Concerts Presents: Ulysses Quartet
description: The Ulysses Quartet celebrates 10 years after their New York debut with a program of Debussy and Mendelssohn. The concert includes a world premiere of Tina Davidson's "Just Before Light."

name: An Evening with Jeff Dye
description: A special headlining performance by nationally touring comedian Jeff Dye in the main room.

name: Sunday Musicians Open Mic
description: An open mic night specifically for musicians hosted by Braden Palmer. Showcase your talent or enjoy local live music in a unique Brooklyn tasting room.

name: Nyc Wjf Jazz Talks
description: An insightful discussion featuring artists exploring themes of witness and resistance through jazz.

name: Connor and Yvette Work It Out
description: Connor Ratliff and Yvette Nicole Brown bring their unique comedic chemistry to the stage.
short_name: Connor and Yvette Work It Out

name: VIP Bowling Lane Add On - Come Back to Earth
description: Reserve a lane during this special tribute to the artistry of Mac Miller.
short_name: VIP Bowling Lane Add On - Come Back to Earth

name: Organizing 101
description: A foundational training session teaching the core skills and principles of effective community organizing.

name: Hybrid Court Watching Training
description: Learn how to observe and document court proceedings to hold the legal system accountable.

name: Ec: Christopher Maclaine
